title: delegate.py: --model opus-4-7/sonnet-4-6 fails with installed claude CLI 2.1.x
source_context: Running OPRK-001 dispatch via cto-orchestrator from /Users/elmo.asmussen/Projects/CTO/OpenRick. Effort 'medium' triggered model arg 'opus-4-7' which the CLI rejects with HTTP 404.
description: When delegate.py is called with --model opus-4-7 (or sonnet-4-6), the spawned 'claude -p --model opus-4-7' subprocess fails because the installed claude CLI (2.1.126) only accepts short aliases (opus/sonnet/haiku). Worse: the actual error 'There's an issue with the selected model (opus-4-7)' is emitted inside the stream-json stdout, but delegate.py reports stderr only ('(no stderr)'), making this nearly impossible to debug from the user's perspective. Reproduces 100% on macOS with claude 2.1.126.
proposed_changes: Two fixes: (1) Map versioned aliases (opus-4-7, sonnet-4-6) to either short aliases (opus, sonnet) or full IDs (claude-opus-4-7, claude-sonnet-4-6) in delegate.py before passing to claude CLI. Probe claude --version and choose accordingly. (2) When subprocess returncode != 0, also surface any stream-json 'error' or 'is_error' events from stdout, not just stderr — current behavior loses the real error message.
